titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Lawson to Director General, Army Veterinary Service, 18 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Lawson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter of recommendation for Patrick Alphonsus Carroll.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Director-General, A.V.S.,
War Office,
London, S,W. With reference to your No. 1045/16 (V.D),
dated 11.1.16, herewith application of Mr P. A. Carroll,
M. R. C. V. S., Carlow, for a temporary commission in the
Army Veterinary Corps, which is recommended. E.J. Lawson , Major, Lr. Castle Yard,
Dublin, 18.1.16
D. A. B. V. S., Irish Command.
To His Majesty's Principal Secretary of State for War. I Patrick Alphonsus Carroll
of Carlow Ireland
being qualified to practice veterinary medicine and surgery, and being registered as
a member of the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons, hereby offer to serve as a
Veterinary Surgeon to His Majesty's Forces, whether within or without the
United Kingdom on the following conditions: 1.The period of my service hereunder shall commence from the date of my
joining for duty, and shall continue until the termination of the War or until my
services are no longer required, whichever shall first happen. 2. My pay and allowances shall, for my first year of service, be the same as
as received by a Lieutenant, Army Veterinary Corps. 3. On completion of twelve months satisfactory service I shall, if my
services are further required, receive an advance of pay to 17/- a day together
with the temporary rank and allowances of a Captain, Army Veterinary Corps. 4. During the said period I will devote my whole time and professional skill
to my service hereunder. 5. In case I shall complete my service hereunder to your satisfaction In all
respects, I shall receive at the end of the said period a gratuity of sixty days.
full pay, in respect of each year or part of a year of service should my employment
be terminated during a year at the rate hereinbefore specified, but in case
I shall in any manner misconduct myself, or shall be (otherwise than through
illness or unavoidable accident) unfit in any respect for service hereunder, of which
misconduct or unfitness you shall be sole judge, you shall be at liberty from and
immediately after such misconduct or unfitness to discharge me from further
service hereunder, and thereupon all pay and allowances shall cease,
and I shall not be entitled to any gratuity in respect of the year of service
during which such misconduct or unfitness occurs.
Dated this 18th day of January 1916 P.A.Carroll (here sign)
Witness to the Signature of the said
Patrick Alphonsus Carroll
E.J. Lawson Major (Witness).
D.A.D.V.S. L.C.
On behalf of the Secretary of State, I accept the forgoing offer.
R. Pringle
Director-General, Army Veterinary Service.
